diagrams.title: Memory Layout of argv and argc on Stack
diagrams.description: Shows how argc and argv are laid out in memory when main() is called
elements: Separate string storage area showing actual argument text
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how the operating system arranges command-line arguments in memory before transferring control to main()

diagrams.title: Program Execution Flow - Command Line to main()
diagrams.description: Shows the journey of arguments from shell to main() function
elements: User types command at shell prompt
Kernel sets up process address space
Kernel transfers control to program entry point (_start)
diagrams.purpose: Understand the complete path of command-line argument processing from user input to program execution

diagrams.title: Exit Status Flow Diagram
diagrams.description: Shows how exit status flows from program to parent process
elements: Value passed to exit() or implicitly returned
Kernel stores exit status in process table
Parent calls wait() to retrieve status
diagrams.purpose: Trace how the return value from main() propagates back to the calling environment
